metadata.title: [REVIEW] Missing data normalization layer violates service architecture pattern

output.summary: Task execution completed with exit code 0
output.implementationNotes:
Task execution completed. Check logs for details.

Final output:
Findings
- High: `normalizeNoteData` doesn’t unwrap `{ data: [...] }` payloads, so `fetchNotesByProject/Task/Customer` can return a non-array; `noteService` then treats it as empty and wipes pagination, breaking notes lists if the backend sends wrapped responses. `src/api/notes.js:14-31` and `src/services/noteService.js:80-108`.
- Medium: `createCustomer` builds an idempotency header but `dataService.post` drops the third argument, so the header is never sent and retries can create duplicates. `src/api/customersBackend.js:171-177` and `src/services/dataService.js:186-193`.

Open questions / assumptions
- Does the notes API ever return `{ data: [...], pagination: ... }`? If yes, the current normalization will empty out note lists.
- Should `dataService` support request config (headers) or should callers use a different client when they need headers?

Change summary
- No code changes (review only).

Tests
- `npm test -- src/errors/__tests__/noteErrors.test.js` (passes; emits console.error from `withErrorHandling` as expected).
